inputs.userGoal: Create the cheapest possible infrastructure plan for dev, staging, and production environments on Azure — web app with API backend, SQL database, Redis cache, and observability. Default region: westus.

reasoning.whyChosen: Free tier: 500 MB/day ingestion, 7-day retention, $0 cost. Sufficient for dev/staging. For prod, consider upgrading to PerGB2018 if you need >7 days retention or >500 MB/day.
reasoning.tradeoffs: 7-day retention and 500 MB/day cap. No alerts, no data export. Upgrade to PerGB2018 if these limits are hit.

resources.name: appi-webapp-{env}-001
resources.sku: workspace-based (free via Log Analytics Free tier)

reasoning.whyChosen: App Insights pricing is entirely driven by the linked Log Analytics workspace. With Free tier workspace, first 500 MB/day is free. Constraint: must link to Log Analytics workspace via WorkspaceResourceId.
alternativesConsidered: Skip observability entirely (not recommended — debugging blind)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Subject to the 500 MB/day and 7-day retention of the Free workspace. No cost for basic APM.

resources.dependencyReasoning: App Insights sends telemetry to the Log Analytics workspace.

reasoning.whyChosen: Standard tier: $0.03/10K operations for secrets — effectively free for small workloads. No monthly base cost. RBAC authorization for governance. Min soft-delete retention (7 days) to minimize locked-resource overhead.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier is already the cheapest. Premium adds HSM keys ($1/key/month) — not needed.

reasoning.whyChosen: Logical SQL server is free — only databases cost money. Public access enabled across all envs to avoid Private Endpoint costs. Entra-only auth still enforced (no extra cost).
alternativesConsidered: Private Endpoints ($7.30/month per endpoint — skipped for cost)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Public access is less secure but saves Private Endpoint + VNet costs. Use firewall rules to restrict IP access.

resources.name: sqldb-webapp-{env}-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Basic DTU is the absolute cheapest SQL Database tier: 5 DTU, 2 GB max size, ~$4.90/month. Sufficient for low-traffic dev/staging and light prod workloads. Local backup redundancy is cheapest. Constraint: must be child of parent SQL Server; location must match.
alternativesConsidered: GP_S_Gen5_1 Serverless (~$5.17/month min with auto-pause — slightly more but scales)
reasoning.tradeoffs: 5 DTU is very limited — suitable for <100 concurrent users. Upgrade to S0 or Serverless GP if performance is insufficient.

resources.dependencyReasoning: SQL Database is a child resource of SQL Server — location must match, server must exist first.

reasoning.whyChosen: Basic C0 is the cheapest Redis tier: 250 MB, no SLA, no replication, ~$16/month. Acceptable for all envs when cost is the priority. Constraint: sku.family must be 'C' for Basic tier.
alternativesConsidered: Skip Redis entirely (saves $16/month but increases DB load)
reasoning.tradeoffs: No SLA, no replication, no persistence. Data loss on node failure. Acceptable for caching (not session state in prod).

reasoning.whyChosen: F1 Free tier: $0/month, 1 GB disk, 60 min/day compute. The absolute cheapest option. Constraint: reserved=true required for Linux. No custom domains, no SSL, no always-on, no deployment slots, no VNet integration.
alternativesConsidered: B1 Basic (~$13/month — adds SSL, custom domains, always-on)
D1 Shared (~$10/month — adds custom domains but still no SSL)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Major limitations: 60 min/day compute cap means app will stop responding after the daily limit. No HTTPS (only *.azurewebsites.net with platform SSL). No deployment slots. Upgrade to B1 for a real workload.

resources.name: app-webapp-{env}-001
resources.sku: Inherited from App Service Plan (F1 Free)

reasoning.whyChosen: App Service on Free tier — $0 compute cost. System-assigned managed identity for passwordless access to SQL, Redis, Key Vault (no extra cost). alwaysOn=false required on Free tier. Constraint: must be in same region as App Service Plan; Linux app needs Linux plan (reserved: true).
alternativesConsidered: Container Apps Consumption (free allowance but more complex)
Azure Functions Consumption (pay-per-execution but not suited for web server)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Free tier has 60 min/day compute limit and cold starts. Sufficient for testing/demo. For real workloads, upgrade the App Service Plan to B1.

resources.dependencyReasoning: App references the App Service Plan (serverFarmId), App Insights (connection string), and Key Vault (for secrets). Managed identity enables passwordless access.

overallReasoning.summary: Absolute lowest-cost three-environment web app architecture. All environments use identical cheapest SKUs — differentiate by upgrading staging/prod SKUs when ready. Estimated monthly cost per environment: ~$21 (SQL Basic $5 + Redis Basic C0 $16 + everything else free/near-free). Total across 3 environments: ~$63/month.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Prioritized cost over resilience. No zone redundancy, no SLA on Redis, no deployment slots, Free App Service Plan has 60 min/day compute cap. This is a development/testing baseline — upgrade SKUs for production workloads.

plan.validation: All 8 resources deploy to westus (single region, no cross-region dependencies). App Service Plan uses reserved=true for Linux matching the app kind=app,linux. SQL Database is child of SQL Server (same location). App Insights links to Log Analytics workspace. Redis sku.family='C' matches Basic tier. Key Vault uses RBAC authorization. All names follow CAF prefix conventions and respect character/length constraints. Each environment deploys into its own resource group (rg-webapp-dev, rg-webapp-staging, rg-webapp-prod).
architecturePrinciples: Secure by default (TLS 1.2+, managed identity, RBAC, Entra-only SQL auth)
Observable (App Insights + Log Analytics Free tier)
Infrastructure as Code ready (parameterized for multi-env deployment)
Upgrade path clear (SKU upgrades only, no architecture changes needed)
